visualGoal: Make a first-time buyer feel the decision is about the ecosystem (rims, QR, catalog, console) rather than the base's torque number. Real wheelbase product proof throughout, one per brand at minimum; no procedural art. Reinforce the three tribes visually: MOZA ladder, Fanatec PS5+catalog, Simagic PC value.

realProductPhotosNeeded.notes: The Fanatec sweet-spot base in the head-to-head table. Currently text-only because no verified image; the DD+ (.webp) IS verified and carries the Fanatec sweet-spot visual instead. A real DD image would let the head-to-head table show all three brands with product art.

The 12Nm Simagic in the head-to-head table. Text-only; the Pro (18Nm) and Ultra (28Nm) images ARE verified and anchor the Simagic visual. Standard would round out the sweet-spot comparison if promoted.
Fanatec entry base; text-only in lineup prose. Low priority for this comparison.
Simagic entry; mentioned in range prose only. Low priority.
Not featured in this three-brand comparison (Logitech is a fourth ecosystem). Only relevant if the scope expands. logitech-rs50-system.jpg IS verified if a Logitech visual is ever wanted.
Flagship row in the glance table only; text-only, low priority.

editorialCompositePrompts.prompt: Realistic sim-racing bench on dark slate under a single dramatic overhead light, three empty 4-bolt mounting plates arranged left-to-right for real wheelbase product overlays (MOZA, Fanatec, Simagic), amber and steel tones, cable tray visible, curator Duke Alvarez standing behind gesturing across the three mounts as if weighing a decision, no fake branded hardware, no fake UI, 16:9 editorial cover.
editorialCompositePrompts.negativePrompt: fake logos, fake wheelbase, warped controls, impossible ergonomics, tiny unreadable text, procedural gradient art

infographicPrompts.title: Which ecosystem should you buy into?
content: Race on Xbox? -> MOZA R3 or Fanatec CSL DD
Pure PC, max feel/$? -> Simagic Alpha Evo
infographicPrompts.style: IgnitionSim command-center decision tree, large readable branches, racing colors, brand product photos as leaf-node markers for Codex overlay

infographicPrompts.title: The 12Nm sweet spot, three ways
content: Simagic Alpha Evo Std ~$548 / 12Nm / open QR-A
Fanatec ClubSport DD ~$630-700 / 15Nm firmware / widest catalog
infographicPrompts.style: Three-column comparison card, product-photo placeholders per column for Codex overlays, QR-lock-in and console icons

curatorScenePrompts.prompt: Realistic adult Gen X sim-racing curator Duke Alvarez in a clean garage cockpit bay, arms crossed, standing between two rigs wearing different-brand wheelbases, confident mechanic-who-has-owned-them-all energy, product overlay space beside him, practical not fantasy, 4:5 portrait.

openQuestions: Confirm current US street prices (all sale-dependent): ClubSport DD ($629.99 sale vs $699.99), ClubSport DD+ (~$899.99), GT DD Pro bundle (~$749.99 5Nm bundle), MOZA R12 ($399 sale / $469 reg), MOZA R21 ($699 sale / $899 reg).
Verify Simagic Alpha Evo Ultra is shipping (not preorder) before treating it as a live buy pick.
Should the head-to-head sweet-spot table show all three brands with product art? Needs real fanatec-clubsport-dd.jpg and/or simagic-evo.jpg (12Nm standard) downloads.
Confirm MOZA 24-month warranty applies across the full R5-R21 range, not just the R3.
